_meta.note: One-line plain-English summaries of each clause in data/contracts/*.md, keyed by supplier id then clause number. Summaries faithfully reflect the real clause text; no terms are invented.

SUP-001.1: Three-year term from January 2023, auto-renewing in 12-month periods unless either side gives 180 days' written notice.
SUP-001.2: Force majeure is written narrowly — it excludes the disruptions most likely to hit this supplier (plant breakdown, energy/material shortages, strikes, shipping delays), so they stay liable but we have little practical remedy; claims need notice within 5 business days.
SUP-001.3: Standard replenishment lead time is 16 weeks — already longer than our 10-week stock gap.
SUP-001.4: We commit to buying at least 80% of the prior year's volume each year (minimum annual offtake).
SUP-001.5: An exclusivity clause bars us from qualifying any alternative source without the supplier's written consent.
SUP-001.6: Prices are fixed annually; the supplier may pass through documented raw-material index moves above 8% on 60 days' notice.
SUP-001.7: Liability is capped at the purchase-order value with no late-delivery penalty — we absorb the full cost of any line-down.
SUP-001.8: Termination only for material breach uncured after 60 days; we have no right to terminate for convenience during the term.
SUP-001.9: Notices in writing; governed by German law with arbitration seated in Frankfurt.

SUP-002.1: Two-year term from March 2023, auto-renewing in 12-month periods unless either side gives 90 days' notice.
SUP-002.2: Broad 'beyond reasonable control' force majeure (including export-control or licensing action), with notice required within 10 days — wider protection for the supplier than the German contract.
SUP-002.3: Standard replenishment lead time is 10 weeks from a confirmed order.
SUP-002.4: No minimum annual offtake commitment — we are free to vary volumes.
SUP-002.5: Explicitly non-exclusive — we keep the right to qualify and buy equivalent scroll compressors from other suppliers at our discretion.
SUP-002.6: Prices are fixed for each 12-month period and reviewed at renewal.
SUP-002.7: Liability capped at the purchase-order value, but with a 0.3%-per-week late-delivery penalty up to an 8% cap — unlike SUP-001, late delivery carries damages.
SUP-002.8: We can terminate for convenience on 90 days' notice; either side can terminate for material breach uncured after 60 days.
SUP-002.9: Notices in writing; governed by Singapore law with arbitration in Singapore (a neutral seat).

SUP-003.1: Three-year term from July 2022, auto-renewing in 12-month periods unless either side gives 90 days' notice.
SUP-003.2: Broad force majeure that expressly includes supplier plant failure, energy interruption, supply-chain disruption and transport delays, with 15 days' notice and a duty to mitigate — far wider cover for the supplier than SUP-001.
SUP-003.3: Standard replenishment lead time is 12 weeks from a confirmed order.
SUP-003.4: We commit to a minimum annual offtake of 75% of the prior year's volume.
SUP-003.5: We may second-source non-proprietary parts, but the proprietary controller firmware and the premium models using it stay sole-source to this supplier.
SUP-003.6: The supplier must maintain and annually review a documented Business Continuity Plan; not having a current plan is a material breach — a contractual resilience lever in our favour.
SUP-003.7: Liability capped at 2x the purchase-order value, with a 0.5%-per-week late-delivery penalty up to a 10% cap — the strongest remedies of the four contracts.
SUP-003.8: Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 45 days.
SUP-003.9: Notices in writing; governed by Italian law with arbitration seated in Milan.

SUP-004.1: Three-year term from September 2022, auto-renewing in 12-month periods unless either side gives 90 days' notice.
SUP-004.2: Force majeure covers acts of God, war, government action and transport/logistics disruption, with 10 days' notice and a duty to mitigate.
SUP-004.3: Standard replenishment lead time is 9 weeks from a confirmed order.
SUP-004.4: We commit to a minimum annual offtake of 70% of the prior year's volume.
SUP-004.5: We may qualify and buy equivalent heat exchangers from an alternative supplier on 30 days' notice — second-sourcing is contractually permitted.
SUP-004.6: Prices are fixed annually and reviewed at renewal.
SUP-004.7: Liability capped at the purchase-order value, with a 0.4%-per-week late-delivery penalty up to an 8% cap.
SUP-004.8: Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 60 days.
SUP-004.9: Notices in writing; governed by German law with arbitration seated in Frankfurt.
